288,Epic,Legal Rights & Compliance,"Sprint 15 delivers the full Legal Service replacing the Sprint 8 stub: rights tags on script versions (option, purchase, underlying rights), NDA access gates blocking script access without a signed NDA, legal holds preventing deletion of assets under hold, and the complete validateScriptPolicy gRPC replacing the stub that always returned passed. Sprint 16 focuses on security hardening and accessibility: penetration test findings remediation, input validation (Zod on all GraphQL mutations), global API rate limiting, RBAC matrix automated tests, SOC 2 Type I readiness evidence package, database backup verification, load test (500 concurrent users
column2: P99 < 500ms), WCAG 2.2 AA audit and remediation, and Lighthouse score ≥ 90. This epic is the last before GA hardening (Sprint 17-18) and ensures the platform is legally, securely, and accessibly ready for public release. Reference: Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprints 15 and 16, Wiki/11-security-compliance.md.","- validateScriptPolicy blocks publish when rights are missing or NDA not signed

293,User Story,As a legal team member I can enforce rights tags NDA access gates and legal holds on scripts to protect intellectual property and meet contractual obligations,"Rights management is a legal requirement for every professional production. Options, purchases, and underlying rights must be tracked on each script version. NDA gates prevent unauthorized access. Legal holds are required for litigation preservation. The validateScriptPolicy gRPC is the trust anchor for the publish workflow. Reference: Wiki/11-security-compliance.md.","- Rights tags: option, purchase, underlying-rights types
- Rights status visible in script settings panel: 'Option: Active', 'Purchase: Not yet', etc.
- NDA gate: unsigned writers see 'Sign NDA to access this script' modal
- Legal hold: held scripts show 'Legal Hold — contact legal@company.com' indicator
- validateScriptPolicy full: returns violations array for publish-saga
column2: blocks lock when rights missing or NDA incomplete",1,New,ScriptOS,ScriptOS\Sprint 15,legal

column1: 294,User Story,As a platform user I have the right to export all my data or delete my account with GDPR compliance and my AI ledger entries are anonymised not deleted,"GDPR (Articles 15 and 17) requires data portability and right to erasure. However, WGA compliance requires AI ledger entries to remain for auditing purposes — deletion is not allowed. Anonymisation (replacing user_id with a pseudonymous hash) satisfies both requirements. Reference: Wiki/11-security-compliance.md.","- 'Download my data' button in account settings produces JSON download within 60s
- JSON export includes: user profile, scripts authored (metadata only), collaboration sessions, AI ledger entries (with user_id anonymized to hash)
- Account deletion: confirmation modal with 30-day grace period notice
- AI ledger entries after deletion: user_id = SHA-256(userId + salt), email = '[deleted]'
- Audit log entry created on data export and on account deletion initiation",2,New,ScriptOS,ScriptOS\Sprint 15,gdpr

295,User Story,As a security team member the platform passes an external penetration test and a 500-user load test with no critical findings and P99 under 500ms,"A failed pentest before GA is a showstopper. A P99 > 500ms under moderate load indicates architectural issues that must be resolved before production scaling. SOC 2 Type I is required for enterprise sales. All three outcomes must be verified before GA is declared. Reference: Wiki/11-security-compliance.md.","- External pentest: all critical and high findings resolved before GA
- Input validation: every GraphQL mutation protected by Zod schema
column2: automated test asserts 400 on bad input

column1: - Global rate limit: 1000 req/min per user enforced at HTTP Gateway
- RBAC matrix: automated test covers 7 roles × all protected resources
- Load test (k6 or Gatling): 500 concurrent users, 10 min duration, P99 < 500ms, error rate < 0.1%
- SOC 2 Type I evidence: audit log completeness > 99%, backup restoration tested, key management documented",1,New,ScriptOS,ScriptOS\Sprint 16,security

column1: 297,Task,Create Atlas DDL for legal.rights_tags nda_records legal_holds tables and implement full Legal Service replacing Sprint 8 stub,"In packages/db/schema/legal.hcl: (1) legal.rights_tags: id UUID PK, script_version_id UUID FK, rights_type ENUM(option,purchase,underlying_rights,clearance), status ENUM(active,expired,pending,not_applicable), rights_holder TEXT, expiry_date DATE, notes TEXT, created_by UUID, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. (2) legal.nda_records: id UUID PK, project_id UUID FK, user_id UUID, signed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, document_url TEXT, is_active BOOL DEFAULT true. (3) legal.legal_holds: id UUID PK, resource_type TEXT, resource_id UUID, reason TEXT, held_by UUID, held_at TIMESTAMPTZ, released_at TIMESTAMPTZ. In services/legal/src/grpc/legal.service.ts implement full ValidateScriptPolicy: query rights_tags for script version → if any required rights not active, add violation. Query nda_records for requesting user → if not signed, add violation. Query continuity_assertions_violations for unresolved ERROR severity → add violation. Return { passed: violations.length === 0, violations }. Replaces Sprint 8 stub. NDA gate middleware: gRPC interceptor on ScriptService that calls CheckNDAAccess(userId, projectId) before any script read
column2: returns PERMISSION_DENIED if NDA not signed. Legal hold: middleware on all service delete handlers calls CheckLegalHold(resourceType, resourceId)
column3: returns FAILED_PRECONDITION if hold exists. Integration test: create script version with missing rights tag
column7: sprint-15,,8,As a legal team member I can enforce rights tags NDA access gates and legal holds on scripts

column1: 298,Task,Build Legal Service GraphQL subgraph with RightsTag NDARecord LegalHold types and mutations,"In services/legal/src/graphql/schema.graphql: type RightsTag { id, scriptVersionId, rightsType, status, rightsHolder, expiryDate, notes }. type NDARecord { id, projectId, userId, signedAt, documentUrl, isActive }. type LegalHold { id, resourceType, resourceId, reason, heldBy, heldAt, releasedAt }. type PolicyValidationResult { passed: Boolean!, violations: [PolicyViolation!]! }. type PolicyViolation { code: String!, message: String!, severity: String! }. Mutations: createRightsTag, updateRightsTag, revokeRightsTag
column6: activeHolds(resourceType, resourceId): [LegalHold!]!. Auth: mutations require LEGAL or ADMIN role. Register subgraph. Integration test: rights query resolves after rights tag created.",,1,New,ScriptOS,ScriptOS\Sprint 15,legal

column1: 299,Task,Implement GDPR data export and account deletion cascade with AI ledger anonymisation,"In services/auth/src/gdpr/gdpr.service.ts implement: (1) exportUserData(userId): aggregates data from all services via gRPC fan-out — auth.users profile, script.projects (where user is owner or collaborator), supervisor.takes (by user), ai.ledger_entries (with user_id returned as-is for the data subject's own copy). Writes JSON to temp file
column2: uploads to S3 with presigned download URL (24h expiry)
column3: returns URL. (2) deleteAccount(userId): (a) Set auth.users.deleted_at = NOW(), is_active = false (immediate deactivation). (b) Enqueue background job DeleteUserDataJob. (c) Job runs within 30 days: anonymize auth.users name/email
column5: anonymize ai.ledger_entries SET user_id = SHA-256(userId + DELETION_SALT) WHERE user_id = userId (rows preserved for WGA). (d) Write audit log entry. Add REST endpoints: GET /auth/gdpr/export → triggers export job
column6: GET /auth/gdpr/export/:jobId → download URL when ready. POST /auth/account/delete → initiates deletion with 30-day grace notice. Integration test: export user data
column9: assert user_id anonymized in ledger after job runs.",,2,New,ScriptOS,ScriptOS\Sprint 15,gdpr

column1: 300,Task,Add Zod validation to all GraphQL mutations and implement global API rate limiting at HTTP Gateway,"In each service's GraphQL resolver file: add Zod schema validation at the top of every mutation resolver. Example pattern for ScriptService createScript mutation: import { z } from 'zod'
column2: const CreateScriptInput = z.object({ projectId: z.string().uuid(), title: z.string().min(1).max(500), format: z.enum(['FEATURE','SERIES_EPISODE','SHORT']) })
column3: at resolver start: const validated = CreateScriptInput.parse(args.input)
column4: throws ZodError → caught by GraphQL error handler → returns 400 with validation details. Create packages/common/src/graphql/zod-error-formatter.ts that converts ZodError to GraphQL UserInputError with field-level messages. Apply to ALL mutations across all 15 services. Global rate limiting at HTTP Gateway (Apollo Router): configure with rhai script or plugin: per-user limit 1000 req/min using Redis sliding window
column5: on exceed return 429 with Retry-After: 60 header. Integration test: send 1001 requests in 60s
column6: assert 429 received. Integration test: send invalid UUID to createProject
column11: sprint-16,,8,As a security team member the platform passes an external penetration test and a 500-user load test

column1: 301,Task,Implement RBAC matrix automated test covering all role-resource combinations and run load test with 500 concurrent users,"In tests/security/rbac-matrix.test.ts: define a permission matrix table: rows = 7 roles (owner, admin, writer, producer, supervisor, editor, viewer), columns = all protected resources (createScript, publishScript, editBreakdown, approvePublish, logTake, viewLedger, accessLegal, deleteScript, exportBudget, viewDOOD, manageUsers). For each cell: expected = allow/deny. Run test: for each role, create a JWT with that role, call the corresponding GraphQL mutation/query, assert allow/deny matches matrix. Assert zero unauthorised access paths found (no allow where deny expected). Load test: write k6 script simulating 500 virtual users for 10 minutes — mix of operations: 40% script reads, 30% editor writes (CRDT), 15% breakdown queries, 10% AI suggestions, 5% search. Run against staging. Assert: P50 < 200ms, P95 < 400ms, P99 < 500ms, error rate < 0.1%. Export k6 HTML report as CI artifact.",,1,New,ScriptOS,ScriptOS\Sprint 16,security
column4: sprint-16,,8,As a security team member the platform passes an external penetration test and a 500-user load test

column1: 302,Task,Assemble SOC 2 Type I evidence package and verify all backup restoration procedures,"SOC 2 evidence collection. For each Trust Service Criterion: (1) CC6.1 Logical access: export Auth Service RBAC configuration, SCIM audit log, JWT signing key rotation schedule. (2) CC7.2 System operations: export Grafana alert rules, PagerDuty escalation policy, Prometheus SLO dashboards. (3) CC9.2 Risk management: export threat model document (create in docs/security/threat-model.md), pentest report (redacted), remediation tracking. (4) A1.2 Availability: export uptime report for Alpha/Beta period
column2: SLA commitments. Backup restoration: (a) Postgres: trigger Cloud SQL point-in-time restore to 24h ago on staging
column4: record RTO. (b) Neo4j: restore from Aura backup
column5: verify character/fact data. (c) S3: restore versioned objects to previous version
column6: verify. Document RPO < 5 min, RTO < 15 min. Create docs/soc2/evidence-index.md listing all artefacts. Target: SOC 2 Type I audit engagement letter signed before GA.",,1,New,ScriptOS,ScriptOS\Sprint 16,security
column9: sprint-16,,6,As a security team member the platform passes an external penetration test and a 500-user load test
